content.full_text:
CITY OF IRWINDALE

5050 NORTH IRWINDALE AVENUE • IRWINDALE, CALIFORNIA 91706
(818) 962-3381 FACSIMILE: (818) 962-4209

August 2, 1991

Kathryn E. Donovan, Counsel
Legal Division
California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re: I-88-253

Dear Ms. Donovan:

My name is Pat Miranda; I am a City Councilmember and I own several parcels of property within the City, one or more of which are situated within 2,500 feet of most of the parcels within the City.

Several years ago, a developer (Bircher) developed two parcels of property within the City as to which the City, through its CRA, helped with financing, etc. in a joint venture activity. Since Parcel "A" of the two is situated within 300 feet of my property and would have benefited my property, I did not participate. Even though Parcel "B" is located more than 2,500 feet from my property, I did not participate there either because I felt that there could be a nexus or linkage between the two because of reinvested funds in "A" from "B".

Now: The developer has quitclaimed both Parcel A and B to the Agency due to the recession and the ongoing costs, retaining as to Parcel B an exclusive right-to-sell on behalf of the Agency (and as to which it could receive commissions), and a managerial contract as to Parcel A (as to which it could obtain lease commissions).

MY QUESTION IS: Since the developer now holds no interest in Parcel A or B except possibly as to commissions, and the CRA owns 100% of the two parcels, may I vote as to the sales of Parcel B or the leases as to Parcel A where the

Kathryn E. Donovan, FPPC
August 2, 1991
Page 2


former developer may earn commissions. Neither the leases
on Parcel A or the sales on Parcel B will have any financial
impact upon my property, but I am concerned that the former
developer, with whom I felt I had potential conflicts, may
be now receiving benefits. In other words, when, if ever,
will the original conflict with this developer be over.

Very truly yours,

[illegible signature]

Patricio "Pat" Miranda
Councilmember
City of Irwindale

CRM/PM:cd

sections.question: Since the developer now holds no interest in Parcel A or B except possibly as to commissions, and the CRA owns 100% of the two parcels, may I vote as to the sales of Parcel B or the leases as to Parcel A where the former developer may earn commissions. Neither the leases on Parcel A or the sales on Parcel B will have any financial impact upon my property, but I am concerned that the former developer, with whom I felt I had potential conflicts, may be now receiving benefits. In other words, when, if ever, will the original conflict with this developer be over.
sections.question_synthetic: A city councilmember who previously abstained from voting on developer parcels within 2,500 feet of his property asks whether he may now vote on agency sales and leases of those same parcels now that the developer retains only commission and management fee interests rather than direct ownership, and when his original conflict of interest with the developer will be resolved.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a city official to FPPC counsel, not an FPPC response. No CONCLUSION section is present in the document. The question section is somewhat informal and narrative rather than clearly demarcated. Reference number I-88-253 is provided.

Neither the leases on Parcel A or the sales on Parcel B will have any financial impact upon my property, but I am concerned that the former developer, with whom I felt I had potential conflicts, may be now receiving benefits. In other words, when, if ever, will the original conflict with this developer be over.

embedding.summary: A City of Irwindale councilmember requests FPPC advice regarding whether he can vote on property transactions involving parcels he previously had conflicts with, now that the original developer has transferred ownership to the CRA but retains commission and management fee interests.
